text: **Anna:** Have a nice {day|日|/deɪ/|Have a nice day at work.} at work, Marcia.
**Marsha:** Oh wait, do you have {pen|ペン|/pen/|I need a pen to write.} and {paper|紙|/ˈpeɪpər/|Write on this paper.} I can borrow?
**Marsha:** We are {meeting|会う/集まる|/ˈmiːtɪŋ/|Let's meet at the cafe.} at this restaurant tonight.
**Marsha:** Today is my {birthday|誕生日|/ˈbɜːrθdeɪ/|Happy birthday to me!}
**Anna:** I {gotta|〜しなければならない|/ˈɡɑːtə/|I gotta go now.} go.
**Anna:** I {forgot|忘れた|/fərˈɡɑːt/|I forgot my keys.} Marcia's birthday and I don't get my {paycheck|給料|/ˈpeɪtʃek/|Receive monthly paycheck.} until next week.
**Anna:** I'll make her a {gift|贈り物|/ɡɪft/|Give a gift to friend.}
**Anna:** I'll just have to borrow a couple of things.
**Anna:** Amelia, may I borrow your {stapler|ホチキス|/ˈsteɪplər/|Use stapler to attach papers.}?
**Anna:** I can lend you my stapler, Anna
**Jonathan:** But please {return|返す|/rɪˈtɜːrn/|Return the book to library.} it.
**Anna:** You can {trust|信頼する|/trʌst/|I trust my friend.} me.
**Anna:** One time I {loaned|貸した|/loʊnd/|Loaned my book to friend.} my stapler to the wrong person.
**Anna:** Bye, {stapler|ホチキス/文房具|/ˈsteɪplər/|I need a new stapler.}
**Anna:** Jonathan, can I borrow your {scissors|はさみ|/ˈsɪzərz/|Please pass the scissors.}?
**Anna:** Sorry to {bother|邪魔する/迷惑をかける|/ˈbɑːðər/|I don't want to bother you.} you.
**Jonathan:** Ah yes, I can {lend|貸す|/lend/|Can I lend you my book?} them to you, but you must return them.
**Jonathan:** These {scissors|はさみ|/ˈsɪzərz/|These scissors are sharp.}, they are the {sharpest|最も鋭い|/ʃɑːrpəst/|This knife is the sharpest.} scissors in the office.
**Anna:** Wow, those are {sharp|鋭い/するどい|/ʃɑːrp/|This knife is very sharp.}.
**Anna:** And I'll bring them back tomorrow.
**Marsha:** I love {birthday|誕生日|/ˈbɜːrθdeɪ/|My birthday is in June.} gifts.
**Marsha:** {Interesting|興味深い|/ˈɪntrəstɪŋ/|This book is very interesting.}
**Anna:** Well, I know you love {hats|帽子|/hæts/|I have three hats.} and you need office supplies.
**Anna:** So this is your own office supply hat.
**Marsha:** Wow, that is a lot of office supply
**Anna:** Many people {loaned|貸した|/loʊnd/|He loaned me his car.} or shared their supplies with me.
**Anna:** Some people really love their office supplies.
**Anna:** It has {paper|紙|/ˈpeɪpər/|I wrote on a piece of paper.} pens.

**Anna:** {tape|テープ/粘着テープ|/teɪp/|I need tape to fix this paper.}, {erasers|消しゴム|/ˈɪrəsərz/|I use erasers to remove pencil marks.}, {rubber bands|輪ゴム|/ˈrʌbər bændz/|Rubber bands hold papers together.}, binder clips, paper clips, and a {light|明かり/ライト|/laɪt/|Turn on the light, please.}
**Marsha:** What is the {balloon|風船/バルーン|/bəˈluːn/|The balloon is red and big.} for?
**Anna:** The balloon will help your friends find you.
**Anna:** You get a {seat|席/座席|/siːt/|Take a seat near the window.} in the restaurant.
**Anna:** Sometimes all the {money|お金/通貨|/ˈmʌni/|I need money to buy a gift.} in the world can't buy the perfect {gift|贈り物/プレゼント|/ɡɪft/|This is a gift for you.}
